article_meta.title: Politics Trump signs dozens of executive or

reasoning: The model does a reasonable job identifying genuine bias patterns in the author's own prose. It correctly avoids flagging quoted material from Trump or other sources. The flagged instances are mostly defensible — 'avalanche,' 'wasted no time,' 'erupted in cheers,' 'lucky supporters,' 'historic souvenirs,' and 'stark departure' are all author prose with spin or subjective framing. The 'make good on his many campaign promises' flag is slightly weaker but still defensible. There are some minor issues: the model could have also flagged 'pet projects' (referring to Biden's policies — loaded word choice), 'fentanyl-peddling' (loaded adjective in author prose), 'His systematic dismantling of Biden-era policies' (spin — 'dismantling' is loaded), and 'kneecapping Detroit' (though this may be attributed to Trump's campaign trail argument). The bias type labels are mostly correct — Spin is appropriate for most items. The 'stark departure' as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives is defensible though could also be Spin.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are from the author's own prose. No quoted text from Trump or any other source was incorrectly flagged. Perfect attribution rule compliance.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are defensible. 'Wasted no time' and 'avalanche' are clearly loaded. 'Erupted in cheers' with 'lucky supporters' and 'historic souvenirs' is celebratory framing. 'Stark departure' is a subjective characterization. The last item about 'make good on campaign promises' is slightly weaker as it's somewhat standard journalistic language, but still defensible as spin.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some notable instances: 'pet projects' (referring to Biden's policies — loaded word choice), 'His systematic dismantling of Biden-era policies' ('dismantling' is a loaded verb), and 'violent, fentanyl-peddling Mexican drug cartels' (while factual, the stacking of adjectives in author prose is a framing choice). These are defensible misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Spin is correctly applied to most instances. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for 'stark departure' is defensible but could also be labeled Spin or Word Choice. Overall the labels are reasonable and within the defined taxonomy.
